title: Ragefire Chasm
short_description: a magma-lit cultist den burrowed beneath the orc capital of Orgrimmar
content: A magma-lit warren burrowed beneath the Cleft of Shadow in Orgrimmar, reached through a fissure the orcs leave unguarded only because most who descend never return. Troggs claw at the lower tunnels, but it is the Searing Blade cultists who concern Thrall's warchiefs — Taragaman the Hungerer and the satyr-priest Bazzalan plot demon-worship a stone's throw from his throne. Young Horde warriors are sent down to scour them out, and the smoke that drifts up through the cracks carries the smell of burned cultist robes back into the Valley of Spirits.

title: The Deadmines
short_description: a Defias stronghold carved into the cliffs of Westfall, ruled by a masked kingpin
content: Cut deep into the cliffs of western Westfall, the old gold-mine the Stonemasons abandoned has become Edwin VanCleef's private kingdom. Cheated of payment for rebuilding Stormwind after the orc wars, VanCleef raised the Defias Brotherhood and pulled his bandits underground to build a juggernaut in the cavern's drowned harbor. Sneed's shredder rattles among the felled lumber, Rhahk'Zor cracks heads in the upper galleries, and at the heart of the workings VanCleef himself waits with his daughter Vanessa. Stormwind sends warbands south, but the Defias hold the tunnels still.

title: Wailing Caverns
short_description: damp grottos beneath the Northern Barrens where a druid's nightmare turned his disciples into serpent-things
content: Hidden beneath the dry hills of the Northern Barrens, a network of damp grottos where the druid Naralex sought to dream the land green again. His meditation in the Emerald Dream went wrong; the nightmare leaked out, and his disciples — Lady Anacondra, Lord Cobrahn, Lord Pythas, Lord Serpentis — twisted into serpent-things that prowl the moss-slick passages. Mutanus the Devourer haunts the deepest pool. The druid Ebru sends warbands of Horde adventurers down to wake Naralex without killing him, before the nightmare rises through the roots and reaches Mulgore.

title: Shadowfang Keep
short_description: a moonlit Silverpine fortress overrun by worgen called forth by an archmage who lost control of his summons
content: The Greymane fortress on the Silverpine bluffs, once seat of Baron Silverlaine, now a moonlit ruin loud with howling. Archmage Arugal called the worgen out of another world to defend Gilneas; instead they devoured the keep's lord and made the halls their den. The Forsaken creep up from Tarren Mill to root them out, while in the cellars Rethilgore tears at chained prisoners and Wolf Master Nandos leads packs through the moonlit courtyards. Sylvanas wants the keep secured; the worgen have other plans, and so do the ghosts of the old Silverlaine line.

title: Blackfathom Deeps
short_description: a drowned night elf temple in Ashenvale, cracked open by Twilight's Hammer and crawling with naga and cultists
content: A drowned night elf temple beneath the still pool at the head of Ashenvale's coast, sunk during the Sundering and only now broken open by the Twilight's Hammer. Old Gods' cultists wade its flooded shrines beside naga raiders from Lady Vashj's broods, all hunting for what the elves once sealed there. Ghamoo-ra the giant turtle haunts the entry pool; Lady Sarevess commands the naga; Aku'mai, an ancient spawn of the deep, coils in the inner sanctum. The Cenarion Circle is desperate to see the temple cleansed before its sleeper wakes.

title: Lower Blackrock Spire
short_description: the smoke-choked lower halls of Blackrock Spire, held by Blackrock and Spirestone orc clans
content: The smoke-choked lower halls of the great spire that splits the Burning Steppes, held by the orcs of the Blackrock and Spirestone clans under warlord Rend Blackhand's lieutenants. Highlord Omokk drills his troops in the entry chamber, the shadow hunter Vosh'gajin reads bones for the dragon-lords above, and War Master Voone marshals raiding parties to strike at Redridge and Loch Modan. Beyond them the spire climbs into Nefarian's domain. Stormwind has sent warbands here for years; every assault batters the lower keep, and every time the orcs rebuild.

title: Scholomance
short_description: a plague-era necromancer academy in the Western Plaguelands crypts, where every dead student rises to teach the next lesson
content: Beneath Caer Darrow in the Western Plaguelands, in the crypts of House Barov, Kel'Thuzad once trained his first necromancers. Though the lich has flown north to Naxxramas, his pupils still teach in those vaulted classrooms. Darkmaster Gandling lectures behind locked doors; Jandice Barov works illusions in her gallery; Ras Frostwhisper, Kel'Thuzad's frostbound apprentice, presides over the great hall. The Argent Dawn pays well for the heads of the masters and the burning of their grimoires. Every dead student rises again; the school never quite empties.

title: Stratholme
short_description: the culled city of the Eastern Plaguelands, split between Scourge undead and Scarlet Crusade fanatics
content: The city Arthas culled before he sailed for Northrend, still burning quietly in the Eastern Plaguelands a decade later. Its eastern half belongs to the Scourge, where Baron Rivendare commands his deathchargers and the dreadlord Balnazzar wears stolen flesh; its western half is held by the Scarlet Crusade, who burn the dead and the dying with equal zeal. The Mograine son walks these streets in mourning for his father's family — slaughtered here. The Argent Dawn watches both wings, and Tirion Fordring's name is spoken at the gates by those who remember Mograine the Ashbringer.

title: Onyxia's Lair
short_description: a sulfurous cavern in Dustwallow Marsh where Deathwing's daughter broods above her clutch of unborn wyrms
content: A cavern of slow, sulfurous air opening off the Dragonmurk in Dustwallow Marsh, where the black brood-mother Onyxia coils above clutches of her unborn. Daughter of Deathwing, she wore human skin in Stormwind for years as Lady Katrana Prestor, whispering ruin into Bolvar Fordragon's ear before Varian Wrynn returned and her mask broke. Driven from the throne city, she has retreated here to mother new wyrms. War parties from both the Alliance and Horde descend the marsh-paths to slay her — none who succeed forget the sound of her wings beneath the stone.

title: Molten Core
short_description: the fiery elemental heart beneath Blackrock Mountain where Ragnaros the Firelord was bound by his own power
content: Down beneath Blackrock Mountain, past Blackrock Depths and through the Dark Iron summoning chamber, lies the burning heart where Ragnaros the Firelord was bound when the Dark Iron emperor Thaurissan summoned him from the Elemental Plane. The Firelord rose, smashed Thaurissan to ash, and now reigns in fire, served by his lieutenants Lucifron, Magmadar, Gehennas, Garr, Sulfuron Harbinger, and Majordomo Executus. The Hydraxian Waterlords of Azshara fund any war party willing to descend, knowing that if Ragnaros breaks free again no shore will be safe.

title: Blackwing Lair
short_description: the crown of Blackrock Spire, where Deathwing's son Nefarian tortures dragonflights into chromatic abominations
content: At the very crown of Blackrock Spire, above the orc-held lower halls, Nefarian — son of Deathwing, brother to Onyxia — runs the black brood's foulest experiments. He chains red, blue, green, and bronze drakes to his laboratory benches and tortures their blood into chromatic wyrms. Razorgore the Untamed broods over a stolen clutch of dragon eggs; Vaelastrasz the Red lies enslaved, begging to be killed; Nefarian himself paces the upper terrace, where the dragonflights' four leaders all want him dead. Few war parties ever reach his hall, fewer return.

title: Naxxramas
short_description: the Lich King's flying necropolis drifting over Dragonblight, crewed by undead champions across four plague-steeped wings
content: Kel'Thuzad's flying necropolis — a black ziggurat that once hung above Stratholme as the Scourge plague was loosed on Lordaeron, now drifting over Dragonblight as the lich-prince marshals his strength for the Lich King. Its four wings each answer to a champion of the dead: Anub'Rekhan and Maexxna in the spider quarter; Noth and Heigan in the plague vats; Instructor Razuvious and the Four Horsemen in the death knight halls; Patchwerk and Thaddius among the construct-pens. Sapphiron coils in the frostwyrm lair below Kel'Thuzad's throne. The Argent Crusade has marked it for fall.

title: Ulduar
short_description: an ancient titan-citadel in the Storm Peaks built to imprison the Old God Yogg-Saron beneath its foundations
content: High in the Storm Peaks, a titan-citadel older than recorded memory, raised to bind the Old God Yogg-Saron beneath its foundations. Its watchers — Hodir of the storm-halls, Thorim of the thunder-throne, Freya of the green wilds, Mimiron of the forge-vaults — once stood vigil together. Loken's betrayal poisoned them; the prisoner whispers up through the floor and the keepers have turned against one another. Algalon the Observer waits in the chamber above to call down the makers' judgement if the prison falls. Brann Bronzebeard begs every warband he meets to march on Ulduar before the Old God wakes fully.

title: Blackrock Depths
short_description: the buried Dark Iron dwarven capital beneath Blackrock Mountain, seat of Emperor Dagran Thaurissan's forges and prisons
content: The Dark Iron city under the mountain, a buried capital of forges, prison cells, arenas, and beer halls answering to Emperor Dagran Thaurissan in his Throne of Thaurissan. His consort Moira Bronzebeard — daughter of Magni, whom Ironforge mourns and seeks — sits beside him, by some accounts willingly. Lord Incendius hammers blades in the forge, Bael'Gar boils molten iron from the runeforge, and the Grim Guzzler tavern serves both Dark Iron and the occasional dwarven spy. Ironforge has wanted Dagran's head since before the Second War; few of its agents return.

title: Dire Maul
short_description: the shattered Highborne ruin of Eldre'Thalas in Feralas, divided among Gordok ogres, corrupted treants, and spectral elven courts
content: The night elf ruin Eldre'Thalas, now called Dire Maul, sprawls across central Feralas in three broken wings. Once a seat of Highborne sorcery before the Sundering, its libraries and palaces are now infested by Gordok ogres who crown their strongest as king — currently King Gordok in the western commons. The eastern Warpwood quarter rots with corrupted treants under the satyr Immol'thar; the northern Capital Gardens hold the spectral courts of the long-dead Highborne, Prince Tortheldrin among them, still feeding on demonic essence to keep their immortal lives going. Cenarion druids pick through what they can save.

title: Maraudon
short_description: the burial grottos of Desolace's centaur tribes, prison of Princess Theradras and haunt of the demigod Zaetar's ghost
content: Hidden in the Valley of Spears in central Desolace, the burial grottos of the centaur tribes — and the prison of Princess Theradras, daughter of the earth-mother Therazane. Long ago she lured the demigod Zaetar, son of Cenarius, into love; their misshapen children became the centaur, and Theradras strangled Zaetar in grief and shame. His ghost still haunts the caverns. Noxxion seeps from the poisoned pools, Razorlash tears at intruders in the green corridor, and Theradras herself rages in the crystal heart of the mountain. The centaur consider the place holy; outsiders consider it cursed.

title: Sunken Temple
short_description: a drowned troll temple in the Swamp of Sorrows where exiled Atal'ai cultists labor to raise their blood god
content: The drowned temple of Atal'Hakkar, sunk by the green dragonflight into the Swamp of Sorrows ages ago to keep what was bound there from rising. The Atal'ai trolls, exiled when Zul'Gurub cast out the Hakkari cult, found it and have been digging ever since to wake their blood god. Jammal'an the Prophet leads the rites; the green dragon Eranikus, once warden of the place, lies twisted into a nightmare-shade in its inner sanctum. If the Avatar of Hakkar manifests fully here, the swamp will not hold him. The Emerald Dragonflight watches, grieving, from above.

title: Zul'Farrak
short_description: an ancient Sandfury troll city baking in the Tanaris desert, ruled by witch doctors and their captive hydra
content: An ancient Sandfury troll city baking under the Tanaris sun, its step-pyramids and dry fountains held by Chief Ukorz Sandscalp and his witch doctors. The trolls call up the hydra Gahz'rilla from the deep well in their gladiatorial pit, and Theka the Martyr leads scarab swarms across the open plaza in numbers that have overrun more than one war party. A captive party of Sandfury rebels chained near the pyramid steps will fight beside whoever frees them. The Steamwheedle Cartel pays bounties for Sandfury scalps to keep Gadgetzan's caravans alive.

title: Zul'Gurub
short_description: the ruined Gurubashi capital deep in Stranglethorn Vale, where high priests labor to call back a blood god who once devoured them
content: The capital of the Gurubashi troll empire, ruined since the time the empire shattered but still beating like a sick heart in the depths of Stranglethorn Vale. The high priests of the old animal gods — Jeklik the bat, Venoxis the serpent, Marli the spider, Thekal the tiger, Arlokk the panther — labor to call back Hakkar the Soulflayer, the blood god the Gurubashi worshipped before he devoured them. Bloodlord Mandokir patrols the terraces with his raptor Ohgan. The Zandalari sail across the South Seas to stop the rite; they hire war parties cheap, and pay only the survivors.

title: Ruins of Ahn'Qiraj
short_description: the outer qiraji city in Silithus, cracked open when the Scarab Wall was broken and swarming with insectoid warriors
content: Beyond the Scarab Wall in southern Silithus, the outer city of the qiraji insectoid empire — sealed since the War of the Shifting Sands by the bronze dragon Anachronos and the night elf general Fandral Staghelm a thousand years ago, broken open again when the great gong was struck. Kurinnaxx the silithid lord rules the dunes; General Rajaxx leads waves of qiraji warriors against the Cenarion outpost; Ossirian the Unscarred, an enslaved tol'vir guardian, paces the outer terraces. The Cenarion Circle and the Brood of Nozdormu wage the war here together, and bury their dead in the same sand.

title: Temple of Ahn'Qiraj
short_description: the inner sanctum of the qiraji empire in Silithus, where the Old God C'Thun dreams in the dark beneath twin emperor thrones
content: The inner sanctum past the ruined city, where the Old God C'Thun dreams in the dark and the qiraji emperors do his bidding. The Prophet Skeram preaches at the gate; the twin emperors Vek'lor and Vek'nilash hold council in mirrored thrones; Princess Huhuran swarms with her brood in the hatchery; Viscidus the elemental seethes in the side chamber. Beyond them all, in a vast pit, the great eye of C'Thun opens. Fandral Staghelm has not forgiven the qiraji for his son Valstann, killed in their first war; he wants the temple gutted, whatever the cost.

title: Gnomeregan
short_description: the gnomes' irradiated capital beneath Dun Morogh, seized by a mad tinker after the trogg flood
content: Carved into the mountains of Dun Morogh, the gleaming gnome capital was lost in a single desperate hour. When troggs boiled up from the deep tunnels, Sicco Thermaplugg convinced High Tinker Mekkatorque to flood the halls with radiation to purge them — a betrayal that poisoned far more gnomes than troggs and left Thermaplugg ruling a glowing ruin. The survivors fled to Ironforge's Tinker Town, and ever since the gnomes have dreamed of scrubbing the radiation from their home and pulling the usurper from his throne of gears.

title: Scarlet Monastery
short_description: the fortress-abbey of the fanatic Scarlet Crusade in Tirisfal, besieged by the Forsaken it was built to destroy
content: Within the haunted woods of Tirisfal Glades stands the cloistered abbey of the Scarlet Crusade, zealots who hunt the undead with a fervor that no longer distinguishes the Scourge from the living. Behind its stained-glass and iron gates, High Inquisitor Whitemane keeps vigil over the fallen Highlord Mograine, whose blade she will not let rest, while the Crusade's commanders drill their swordsmen in cloister and chapel. The Forsaken press against its walls from without, for the fanatics within would burn all of Lordaeron to ash to cleanse it. To walk those candlelit halls is to be named heretic on sight, whether one breathes or not.

title: Razorfen Kraul
short_description: a thornbrush warren in the Barrens where the Death's Head quillboar commune with an old god's burning blood
content: In the southern reaches of the Barrens rises a vast warren woven entirely of living thornbrush, raised by the quillboar over generations until it became a fortress of barbed walls. Here the Death's Head clan keeps its rites, and their ancient hag Charlga Razorflank reads omens in the smoldering blood of Agamaggar, the boar-god slain in ages past whose embers still burn beneath the brambles. The quillboar guard their thorny labyrinth with savage devotion, and any who push into the kraul find every path lined with spines and ambush. It is a green and bristling maze that swallows the careless whole.

title: Razorfen Downs
short_description: the quillboar burial mound in the Barrens where Amnennar the Coldbringer has raised a lich's spire among the bones
content: To the south of the thornbrush kraul lies the great burial mound of the quillboar, a hill of bones and bramble where their dead have been laid for untold generations. The Scourge have found it, and from its peak the lich Amnennar the Coldbringer has raised a spire of frozen death, bending the spirits of the boar-folk to his will and turning their sacred ground into a nursery for the undead. Where once the quillboar mourned, now cold winds carry the chittering of risen things across the Downs. The living dead and the betrayed quillboar both stalk its winding paths, and the chill deepens the higher one climbs.

title: Uldaman
short_description: a buried titan vault in the Badlands where Dark Iron dwarves and troggs claw for the secret of dwarven origins
content: Beneath the cracked earth of the Badlands sleeps Uldaman, a vault sealed by the titans in the world's first days and forgotten until the dwarves came digging. Within its stone galleries wait the Discs of Norgannon, records said to hold the truth of how the dwarves were shaped from living rock — a truth the Dark Iron clan would seize for itself, and that the Stonevault troggs, mindless creations spilled from the same vault, blindly defend. Constructs of iron and stone still stir in the deep chambers, guarding their makers' work against all comers. To descend is to walk backward into the prehistory of an entire people.

title: The Stockade
short_description: Stormwind's flooded prison beneath the Canals, seized in a Defias riot under Bazil Thredd
content: Beneath the Canals of Stormwind City lies the Stockade, the human capital's dank prison where the worst of the realm's outlaws are kept behind iron and water. The Defias inmates have risen in open riot, and under the cunning of Bazil Thredd they have overrun their gaolers and turned the flooded cells into a stronghold of their own. The city guard cannot retake the warren alone, and so calls go out for sturdy hands to put down the uprising before the brigands break free into the streets above. It is close, wet, brutal work in the dark under the king's own city.

title: Upper Blackrock Spire
short_description: the high halls of Blackrock Mountain where Rend Blackhand and Lord Victor Nefarius scheme over the black brood
content: High above the orc warrens that honeycomb Blackrock Mountain rise the upper halls of the Spire, where the Dark Horde still clings to power long after the old wars. Here the warlord Rend Blackhand, last of his bloodline to hold a throne, rules a host of orcs, ogres, and trolls who never bowed to Thrall — and at his side stands Lord Victor Nefarius, a sorcerer who is no man at all but the black dragon Nefarian in stolen guise. Together they breed and twist the black brood in hidden roosts, plotting to loose a flight of dragons upon the world. The smoke-choked heights are a den of warlords and wyrms, and only a warband in force dares climb them.

title: Hellfire Ramparts
short_description: the battlements of Hellfire Citadel where Kargath's fel orcs watch over the ruined peninsula
content: The first wall any soul faces upon entering Hellfire Citadel is the Ramparts, a sweep of jagged battlements thrust over the red wastes of the peninsula. Here the Shattered Hand orcs of Kargath Bladefist keep watch, their flesh corrupted to crimson by the fel draughts brewed within, and they loose hellish hounds and warlocks upon any who climb toward the keep. From these heights the whole blasted land can be seen, and from these heights the fel horde rains fire down upon the green road below.

title: The Blood Furnace
short_description: the forge within Hellfire Citadel where the captive pit lord's blood is rendered into fel orcs
content: Deep in Hellfire Citadel, beyond the Ramparts, the orcs keep a furnace fed not by coal but by blood. The pit lord Magtheridon, broken and chained below, is bled drop by drop, and his tainted blood is worked into draught after draught that turns common orcs into red-skinned, fel-maddened killers for Illidan's armies. Magtheridon's own jailers run the works, and the prisoners dragged here do not return.

title: The Shattered Halls
short_description: Kargath Bladefist's inner stronghold within Hellfire Citadel, the last bastion of the Shattered Hand
content: Within the heart of Hellfire Citadel lie the Shattered Halls, the inner sanctum where Kargath Bladefist himself holds court over the Shattered Hand. The corridors run with the blood of captives chained in rows, awaiting the blade or the brewing-vats, and Kargath's most savage warriors stalk the smoke-filled passages with axe and fel-fire. This is the orcs' last redoubt in the peninsula, and to reach the warchief who severed his own hand to escape his shackles is to fight through every horror his clan can muster.

title: The Slave Pens
short_description: the holding cages of Coilfang Reservoir where Vashj's naga drown the broken into bondage
content: Beneath the drowned marsh of Zangarmarsh, the naga of Coilfang Reservoir keep their Slave Pens, a maze of flooded cages and dripping stone. Here Lady Vashj's serpent-folk herd captured Broken draenei and others into chains, breaking their spirits with toil and water until they labor for the great work below. Naga overseers and their summoned tide-creatures patrol the channels, and the cries of the drowning echo down the green-lit halls.

title: The Underbog
short_description: the fungal deep of Coilfang where bog lords and the great hydra Hungarfen rule the spore-stalks
content: Below the cages of Coilfang the stone gives way to the Underbog, a cavernous fen where titanic mushrooms rise like towers and the air hangs thick with spores. Lumbering bog lords and shambling fungal giants wander the murk, and in the deepest pool coils Hungarfen, an ancient hydra whose many heads drag the unwary beneath the muck. The naga suffer these wild things to thrive, for the rot they spread feeds the works that drain the marsh.

title: The Steamvault
short_description: the pumping heart of Coilfang where the naga drain the marsh dry for Vashj's design
content: At the lowest reach of Coilfang Reservoir thrums the Steamvault, a roaring machine-works of pipes, turbines, and scalding mist. Here Lady Vashj's naga labor at the true purpose of the whole drowned complex: to pump the waters of Zangarmarsh away into the void, withering Outland's last green marsh for some design known only to their mistress. Hydromancers and steam-wreathed guardians defend the controls, and the thunder of the pumps never ceases.

title: Mana-Tombs
short_description: a wing of Auchindoun where the Consortium and void-summoners fight among the draenei dead
content: Within the shattered crypt-city of Auchindoun, beneath Terokkar Forest, lie the Mana-Tombs, vaults raised to house the honored draenei dead. Now the place is a battleground: the ethereal traders of the Consortium delve the tombs for the rich arcane charge sealed within, while Nexus-Prince Shaffar's rival ethereals summon creatures of the void to claim it for themselves. Among the resting draenei, ghostly and starlit foes clash over the ruins of a holy place.

title: Auchenai Crypts
short_description: the ossuary of Auchindoun where the draenei death-priests turned to necromancy
content: The Auchenai were the draenei keepers of Auchindoun, sworn to tend the dead and guard the passage of souls. But when the crypt-city was sundered, despair and dark powers turned them from their vigil, and now the Auchenai Crypts ring with necromantic rites where once there were prayers. Exarch Maladaar, once a noble shepherd of the departed, presides over the ossuary as its corrupted lord, raising the very dead he was meant to honor against any who trespass.

title: Sethekk Halls
short_description: the arakkoa wing of Auchindoun where the Sethekk cult worships the raven-god Anzu
content: One wing of broken Auchindoun has fallen to the Sethekk, a sect of the bird-like arakkoa cast out and embittered. In these shadowed halls they bow not to the Light but to Anzu, the raven-god of the cursed, and their talon-priests work dark rites to call their patron back into the world. Feathered fanatics and summoned shadow-ravens haunt the passages, and at their heart the Sethekk seers chant for the wings of their dread deity.

title: Shadow Labyrinth
short_description: the deepest wing of Auchindoun where the Shadow Council whispers and a captive lord of sound is bound
content: The deepest and darkest reach of Auchindoun is the Shadow Labyrinth, a winding maze claimed by the Shadow Council, the warlock cabal that has served the Legion since the orcs' first fall. Through these black corridors their whispers spread corruption, and at the labyrinth's heart they hold Murmur, a vast and ancient lord of sound and silence dragged from beyond the world and chained to do their bidding. To walk here is to be deafened by a being whose merest voice can shatter stone.

title: Old Hillsbrad Foothills
short_description: a passage at the Caverns of Time to the day a young Thrall was meant to remain a prisoner
content: Through the sands of the Caverns beneath Tanaris opens a road into the past, to the green hills of Hillsbrad as they were in years long gone. Here a young Thrall languishes in the internment camp at Durnholde Keep, and history demands that he break free to become the warchief who will lead his people to freedom. The Infinite Dragonflight schemes to murder him in his chains and unravel all that followed, and the bronze guardians beg travelers to shield the slave's escape and keep the timeline whole.

title: The Black Morass
short_description: a wound in time at the Caverns of Time, where the first Dark Portal must be allowed to open
content: Through the swirling sands of the Caverns beneath Tanaris lies a sodden marsh fixed at the moment the Dark Portal first tore open. The bronze dragonflight stands guard while Medivh, possessed and unknowing, works the spell that will let the orcish horde cross into Azeroth — for that doomed hour must happen exactly as it did. The Infinite Dragonflight pours assassins through the rifts to break the timeline, and only by holding the line through wave upon wave can the present be preserved.

title: The Mechanar
short_description: a fortress of Tempest Keep where Kael'thas's blood elves forge arcane war-machines
content: Adrift in the broken sky of Netherstorm hangs the Mechanar, one of the satellite fortresses of Tempest Keep, the stolen vessel of the naaru. Within its humming corridors Kael'thas Sunstrider's blood elves labor to forge arcane war-machines and animate constructs of crystal and steel, all overseen by the cold genius Pathaleon the Calculator. The whole place is a manufactory of the prince's ambition, churning out engines of magic to serve his mad pact with the Burning Legion.

title: The Botanica
short_description: a Tempest Keep arboretum where the blood elves cultivate void-warped flora
content: Another of Tempest Keep's drifting fortresses above Netherstorm is the Botanica, a vast crystalline arboretum given over to the blood elves' arcane horticulture. Here Kael'thas's people graft and nurture flora gathered from a hundred worlds, twisting it with raw magic and void energies until the gardens themselves turn hostile. At the heart of the green-glassed halls looms Warp Splinter, a colossal corrupted ancient grown to be the prince's living weapon.

title: The Arcatraz
short_description: Tempest Keep's prison-ship for cosmic horrors, its wardens slain and its cells flung open
content: The Arcatraz was built by the naaru as a flying prison to hold creatures too dangerous to walk under any sky — abyssal demons, void-things, and worse. When Kael'thas's blood elves seized Tempest Keep, they tore open the cells and freed what slumbered within, chief among them the Harbinger Skyriss, an agent of the void who now schemes to spread madness and dissolution. The shattered wardens drift dead in the halls, and the horrors they once guarded prowl free among the stars.

title: Magisters' Terrace
short_description: Kael'thas's last redoubt on the sunwell isle, where the fallen prince makes his final mad stand
content: On the sun-washed Isle of Quel'Danas, where the broken Sunwell once burned, stands Magisters' Terrace — the final refuge of Kael'thas Sunstrider. Driven from Tempest Keep and wholly given over to his demonic masters, the fallen prince of the blood elves rallies his last loyal magisters here, scheming to call his dread patron Kil'jaeden into the world through the very wellspring of his people's power. His own kin, betrayed and disillusioned, hunt him through the marble halls to end his madness where it began.

title: Karazhan
short_description: the last Guardian's abandoned tower in Deadwind Pass, haunted by the echoes of Medivh's power
content: Rising alone from the dead canyon of Deadwind Pass, the tower of Karazhan was the seat of Medivh, last of the Guardians of Tirisfal. Though its master is long dead, the residue of his immense, possessed power never settled — the halls stage phantom operas that play to the death, a chessboard moves armies of its own accord, and the dread Prince Malchezaar of the Burning Legion has slipped in to claim the leyline nexus for himself. Mages and historians covet what the tower remembers; few who climb it descend.

title: Gruul's Lair
short_description: the cliffside hold of Gruul the Dragonkiller, where the gronn overlord and his son rule the Blade's Edge
content: High in the jagged crags of Blade's Edge Mountains, the gronn called Gruul the Dragonkiller reigns over a brood of monstrous giants. He earned his dread name by slaughtering the black dragons who once nested among these peaks, hanging their impaled corpses as trophies along the ridgelines. At the threshold of his cavern, his maul-wielding son High King Maulgar commands a savage council of ogres, and any who would reach the old gronn must first break that brutish guard.

title: Magtheridon's Lair
short_description: the chained pit lord beneath Hellfire Citadel, once Outland's master, straining against Illidan's bonds
content: Far below the ramparts of Hellfire Citadel, in the bleeding heart of the Hellfire Peninsula, the pit lord Magtheridon hangs in agony upon a frame of arcane chains. Before Illidan came, this demon ruled the shattered remnants of Draenor as its self-styled lord; now he is a captive, his very blood drained to corrupt the orcs into fel-mad servants. He howls and strains against his bonds, and his keepers fight to ensure he is never loosed upon the world again.

title: Serpentshrine Cavern
short_description: the flooded heart of Coilfang where Lady Vashj works Illidan's scheme to drain Outland's waters
content: Deep within the drowned vaults of Coilfang Reservoir, in the marsh-country of Zangarmarsh, lies the sunken sanctum of Lady Vashj. Once handmaiden to Queen Azshara and now the serpentine right hand of Illidan Stormrage, the naga sorceress hoards Outland's failing waters here, throttling the springs that feed the swamp at her master's command. Her coiled court of naga and beasts guards the flooded shrine, and the parched land withers as she works her slow and ruinous design.

title: Tempest Keep
short_description: the naaru satellite-fortress in Netherstorm seized by Kael'thas, who courts the Legion for power
content: Adrift in the broken sky of Netherstorm floats Tempest Keep, a crystalline fortress built by the naaru as a vessel of pure Light. Kael'thas Sunstrider, prince of the blood elves, stormed and stole it, turning its arcane engines toward his own insatiable hunger for magic. From this hijacked stronghold he treats with the agents of the Burning Legion, gathering power for a master far darker than any his ruined people once served.

title: Battle for Mount Hyjal
short_description: a Caverns of Time vision of the World Tree's last defense against Archimonde and the Legion
content: Within the timeless sands of the Caverns of Time, beneath Tanaris, the keepers of history reopen the climax of the Third War upon the slopes of Mount Hyjal. Here the night elves, humans, and orcs stood together in desperate alliance to defend the World Tree from the demon-lord Archimonde and his endless host. Those who walk this vision relive that terrible night — the falling bastions, the rallying defenders, and the final stand at Nordrassil where Archimonde was unmade.

title: Black Temple
short_description: the ruined draenei temple Karabor turned into Illidan's fortress, besieged by Akama and the Aldor
content: In the ashen ruin of Shadowmoon Valley stands the Black Temple, once the radiant draenei sanctuary of Karabor before the orcs defiled it in blood. Now it is the seat of Illidan Stormrage, the Betrayer, who broods upon his throne behind walls of fel iron and shadow. The broken draenei Akama, long forced into Illidan's service, has turned at last — leading the Aldor and the Ashtongue against the very lord he was bound to obey, that the temple might be cleansed.

title: Sunwell Plateau
short_description: the corrupted Sunwell on Quel'Danas, where Kil'jaeden is summoned and the Shattered Sun stand fast
content: Upon the Isle of Quel'Danas, at the desecrated heart of the high elves' ancient Sunwell, the deceiver Kil'jaeden is dragged toward the waking world. The fallen prince Kael'thas spent his last madness to crack the wellspring open as a portal for his demonic master, and through that wound the eredar lord begins to emerge. Against this horror the Shattered Sun Offensive — blood elves and draenei united in last extremity — fight to seal the breach before the deceiver sets foot upon Azeroth.

title: Zul'Aman
short_description: the Amani forest kingdom where Zul'jin wakes the animal-god avatars against the high elves
content: Within the shadowed pines of the Ghostlands lies Zul'Aman, the war-capital of the Amani trolls. Their warlord Zul'jin, scarred and embittered by old defeats at elven hands, has risen again with a furious purpose — to call upon the avatars of the animal-gods, the bear, the eagle, the lynx, and the dragonhawk, and loose their primal fury upon all who trespass his forest. He would drive the high elves from these woods forever, or see his kingdom burn in the trying.

title: Utgarde Keep
short_description: the Vrykul stronghold in Howling Fjord where Ingvar the Plunderer wakes frost-giant kin for the Lich King
content: Carved into the cliffs of the Howling Fjord, the grim hold of Utgarde was raised by the Vrykul, the giant forebears of humankind. Roused from ancient slumber and sworn to the Lich King, these towering warriors arm themselves anew within its frozen forges and crypts. Their captain, Ingvar the Plunderer, drives the host onward, and so steadfast is his oath to the master of the Scourge that death itself does not release him from the fight.

title: Utgarde Pinnacle
short_description: the high summit of Utgarde where King Ymiron and the drake Skadi guard the Vrykul kings' tombs
content: At the windswept crown of the Howling Fjord rises Utgarde Pinnacle, the sacred high seat of the Vrykul and the resting place of their ancient kings. Here the brooding King Ymiron presides over the tombs of his forebears, calling upon their dead majesty to defend the summit. Above the icy battlements wheels Skadi the Ruthless astride his proto-drake, raining death upon any who dare ascend toward the throne of the giant-kings.

title: The Nexus
short_description: Malygos's icebound spire on Coldarra, where the blue dragonflight hoards the world's magic
content: On the frozen island of Coldarra off the Borean coast, the blue dragonflight's spire turns like a vast orrery of ice and arcane light. Malygos, the Spell-Weaver, woke from ten thousand years of madness convinced that mortals had bled Azeroth's magic to ruin, and from here his flight wages a war to reclaim every leyline. Within, the captive consort Keristrasza is tormented into service, and the very walls bleed raw arcane frost upon any who climb toward the Aspect's sanctum.

title: The Oculus
short_description: the floating ring above the Nexus where captured drakes are loosed to fight Malygos's war in the sky
content: Suspended in the bitter air above Coldarra hangs the Oculus, a shattered ring of arcane platforms that orbits the Nexus far below. Here Malygos's flight cages drakes of every brood, breaking them to serve as mounts in the great arcane war he wages across the heavens. Those who would challenge the Spell-Weaver's design must seize these very steeds and take wing, trading blows amid the clouds where the floating ring slowly wheels.

title: Azjol-Nerub
short_description: the upper halls of the old nerubian kingdom, where Scourge spider-lords hold against intruders
content: Beneath the frozen plains of Dragonblight wind the upper passages of Azjol-Nerub, once the proud subterranean empire of the spider-like nerubians. Long ago the Scourge overran this kingdom in a terrible war, and now the Lich King's risen spider-lords skitter through its pillared halls, defending the dark roads that lead ever downward. The carved grandeur of the old empire still stands, though its builders are bent now to the will of death.

title: Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom
short_description: the deeper nerubian dark beneath the ice, where a faceless cult of an old god festers
content: Far below Azjol-Nerub, in the lightless deeps under Dragonblight, lies Ahn'kahet, the Old Kingdom of the nerubians. Here the corruption runs deeper than the Scourge — for the faceless servants of an imprisoned old god, Yogg-Saron, have wormed their way into the ancient halls, spreading a madness that turns even the unliving against one another. Whispers crawl through the dark, and a cult of the void festers in the silence beneath the world.

title: Drak'Tharon Keep
short_description: a fallen troll fortress on the Zul'Drak border, now a Scourge slaughterhouse raising the dead
content: On the cold marches between Grizzly Hills and Zul'Drak stands Drak'Tharon Keep, a brooding stronghold the Drakkari trolls abandoned to the advancing Scourge. Where war-drums once sounded, the undead now toil at a grim harvest, butchering the living and binding the slain into the Lich King's swelling host. The blood of the old garrison still stains its stones, and from its ramparts the dead look out upon a land they could not hold.

title: Gundrak
short_description: the temple-city of the Drakkari ice trolls, who butcher their own animal-gods for power against the Scourge
content: In the frozen troll land of Zul'Drak rises Gundrak, the great temple-city where the Drakkari ice trolls have gathered in their hour of desperation. With the Scourge pressing hard upon their borders, the trolls have turned to a monstrous bargain — slaying their own revered animal-gods upon the altars and devouring their power to win strength enough to survive. Blood runs down the temple steps, and the loa themselves are unmade by the very people who once worshipped them.
